req.title: Immunization record (negative)
req.statement: A clinician shall record an Immunization with administered date not in the future. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for Immunization recording requirement:
"A clinician shall record an Immunization with administered date not in the future."

Covers:
- Happy path: valid immunization with past/today dates
- Negative paths: future date, permission denial, missing mandatory elements,
  conflicting state transitions
"""

import pytest
from datetime import date, datetime, timedelta, timezone
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional
from enum import Enum


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain model stubs (replace with real imports in your codebase)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class ImmunizationStatus(str, Enum):
    COMPLETED = "completed"
    ENTERED_IN_ERROR = "entered-in-error"
    NOT_DONE = "not-done"


class Role(str, Enum):
    CLINICIAN = "clinician"
    PATIENT = "patient"
    ADMIN = "admin"
    READ_ONLY = "read_only"


class PermissionError(Exception):
    """Raised when a user lacks the required privilege."""


class ValidationError(Exception):
    """Raised when a resource fails domain validation."""


class ConflictError(Exception):
    """Raised on illegal state transitions."""


@dataclass
class Vaccine:
    code: str           # e.g. CVX code
    display: str


@dataclass
class Patient:
    id: str
    name: str


@dataclass
class Practitioner:
    id: str
    name: str
    role: Role


@dataclass
class ImmunizationRecord:
    patient: Optional[Patient] = None
    vaccine: Optional[Vaccine] = None
    occurrence_date: Optional[date] = None          # administered date
    status: Optional[ImmunizationStatus] = None
    performer: Optional[Practitioner] = None
    lot_number: Optional[str] = None
    dose_quantity: Optional[float] = None           # mL
    route: Optional[str] = None                     # e.g. "IM"
    site: Optional[str] = None                      # e.g. "left deltoid"
    recorded_by: Optional[Practitioner] = None
    recorded_at: Optional[datetime] = None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Service stub (replace with real service / repository layer)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class ImmunizationService:
    """
    Thin facade; real implementation would persist to FHIR / DB.
    Raises domain exceptions on invalid input.
    """

    REQUIRED_FIELDS = ("patient", "vaccine", "occurrence_date", "status", "performer")

    def record(
        self,
        record: ImmunizationRecord,
        requesting_user: Practitioner,
    ) -> ImmunizationRecord:
        self._check_permission(requesting_user)
        self._validate(record)
        record.recorded_by = requesting_user
        record.recorded_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        return record

    def update_status(
        self,
        existing: ImmunizationRecord,
        new_status: ImmunizationStatus,
        requesting_user: Practitioner,
    ) -> ImmunizationRecord:
        self._check_permission(requesting_user)
        self._check_status_transition(existing.status, new_status)
        existing.status = new_status
        return existing

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # private helpers
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def _check_permission(self, user: Practitioner) -> None:
        if user.role not in (Role.CLINICIAN,):
            raise PermissionError(
                f"User '{user.name}' with role '{user.role}' is not authorised "
                "to record immunizations."
            )

    def _validate(self, record: ImmunizationRecord) -> None:
        # 1. mandatory fields
        missing = [f for f in self.REQUIRED_FIELDS if getattr(record, f) is None]
        if missing:
            raise ValidationError(f"Missing mandatory field(s): {', '.join(missing)}")

        # 2. administered date must not be in the future
        today = date.today()
        if record.occurrence_date > today:
            raise ValidationError(
            f"Administered date {record.occurrence_date} is in the future "
            f"(today is {today})."
        )

    ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS = {
        ImmunizationStatus.COMPLETED: {ImmunizationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR},
        ImmunizationStatus.NOT_DONE: {ImmunizationStatus.COMPLETED,
                                      ImmunizationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR},
        ImmunizationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR: set(),   # terminal state
    }

    def _check_status_transition(
        self,
        current: ImmunizationStatus,
        new: ImmunizationStatus,
    ) -> None:
        allowed = self.ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS.get(current, set())
        if new not in allowed:
            raise ConflictError(
                f"Transition from '{current}' to '{new}' is not permitted."
            )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture()
def service() -> ImmunizationService:
    return ImmunizationService()


@pytest.fixture()
def clinician() -> Practitioner:
    return Practitioner(id="prac-001", name="Dr. Alice Smith", role=Role.CLINICIAN)


@pytest.fixture()
def patient() -> Patient:
    return Patient(id="pat-001", name="John Doe")


@pytest.fixture()
def flu_vaccine() -> Vaccine:
    return Vaccine(code="141", display="Influenza, seasonal, injectable")


@pytest.fixture()
def valid_record(patient, flu_vaccine, clinician) -> ImmunizationRecord:
    """A fully-populated, valid immunization record (yesterday)."""
    return ImmunizationRecord(
        patient=patient,
        vaccine=flu_vaccine,
        occurrence_date=date.today() - timedelta(days=1),
        status=ImmunizationStatus.COMPLETED,
        performer=clinician,
        lot_number="LOT-2024-XYZ",
        dose_quantity=0.5,
        route="IM",
        site="left deltoid",
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Happy-path tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestHappyPath:

    def test_record_immunization_yesterday(self, service, valid_record, clinician):
        """Administered date = yesterday → accepted."""
        result = service.record(valid_record, clinician)
        assert result.recorded_by == clinician
        assert result.recorded_at is not None
        assert result.occurrence_date == date.today() - timedelta(days=1)

    def test_record_immunization_today(self, service, valid_record, clinician):
        """Administered date = today → accepted."""
        valid_record.occurrence_date = date.today()
        result = service.record(valid_record, clinician)
        assert result.occurrence_date == date.today()

    def test_record_immunization_historical_date(self, service, valid_record, clinician):
        """Administered date well in the past (childhood record) → accepted."""
        valid_record.occurrence_date = date(2000, 6, 15)
        result = service.record(valid_record, clinician)
        assert result.occurrence_date == date(2000, 6, 15)

    def test_recorded_at_is_utc(self, service, valid_record, clinician):
        """Timestamp written at record time is timezone-aware UTC."""
        result = service.record(valid_record, clinician)
        assert result.recorded_at.tzinfo is not None

    def test_recorded_by_matches_requesting_user(self, service, valid_record, clinician):
        """The recorder field is stamped with the requesting practitioner."""
        result = service.record(valid_record, clinician)
        assert result.recorded_by.id == clinician.id

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("days_ago", [1, 30, 365, 3650])
    def test_various_past_dates_accepted(
        self, service, valid_record, clinician, days_ago
    ):
        """Parametrised check: any past date is accepted."""
        valid_record.occurrence_date = date.today() - timedelta(days=days_ago)
        result = service.record(valid_record, clinician)
        assert result.occurrence_date < date.today() or \
               result.occurrence_date == date.today()

    def test_optional_fields_are_preserved(self, service, valid_record, clinician):
        """Lot number, dose, route, site survive round-trip unchanged."""
        result = service.record(valid_record, clinician)
        assert result.lot_number == "LOT-2024-XYZ"
        assert result.dose_quantity == 0.5
        assert result.route == "IM"
        assert result.site == "left deltoid"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Negative path – future date
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestFutureDate:

    def test_tomorrow_raises_validation_error(self, service, valid_record, clinician):
        """Administered date = tomorrow → ValidationError."""
        valid_record.occurrence_date = date.today() + timedelta(days=1)
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="future"):
            service.record(valid_record, clinician)

    def test_far_future_raises_validation_error(self, service, valid_record, clinician):
        """Administered date far in the future → ValidationError."""
        valid_record.occurrence_date = date.today() + timedelta(days=365)
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="future"):
            service.record(valid_record, clinician)

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("days_ahead", [1, 7, 30, 180, 365])
    def test_various_future_dates_rejected(
        self, service, valid_record, clinician, days_ahead
    ):
        """Parametrised: every future date must be rejected."""
        valid_record.occurrence_date = date.today() + timedelta(days=days_ahead)
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
            service.record(valid_record, clinician)

    def test_future_date_error_message_contains_date(
        self, service, valid_record, clinician
    ):
        """Error message surfaces the offending date for auditability."""
        future = date.today() + timedelta(days=3)
        valid_record.occurrence_date = future
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match=str(future)):
            service.record(valid_record, clinician)

    def test_future_date_not_persisted(self, service, valid_record, clinician):
        """recorded_at must remain None when validation fails (no partial write)."""
        valid_record.occurrence_date = date.today() + timedelta(days=1)
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
            service.record(valid_record, clinician)
        assert valid_record.recorded_at is None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Negative path – permission denial
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestPermissionDenial:

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("role", [Role.PATIENT, Role.ADMIN, Role.READ_ONLY])
    def test_non_clinician_roles_are_rejected(
        self, service, valid_record, role
    ):
        """Users without CLINICIAN role must be denied."""
        non_clinician = Practitioner(id="usr-999", name="Unauthorised User", role=role)
        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            service.record(valid_record, non_clinician)

    def test_permission_error_message_contains_role(self, service, valid_record):
        """Error message exposes the offending role for the audit log."""
        admin = Practitioner(id="adm-001", name="Bob Admin", role=Role.ADMIN)
        with pytest.raises(PermissionError, match=str(Role.ADMIN)):
            service.record(valid_record, admin)

    def test_permission_checked_before_validation(self, service):
        """Permission gate fires even when the record itself is invalid."""
        bad_record = ImmunizationRecord()   # everything is None
        read_only = Practitioner(id="ro-001", name="Viewer", role=Role.READ_ONLY)
        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):   # NOT ValidationError
            service.record(bad_record, read_only)

    def test_patient_role_cannot_record(self, service, valid_record, patient):
        """A patient user object is not allowed to record immunizations."""
        patient_user = Practitioner(id=patient.id, name=patient.name, role=Role.PATIENT)
        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            service.record(valid_record, patient_user)

    def test_permission_denial_does_not_mutate_record(
        self, service, valid_record
    ):
        """On permission failure the record must remain untouched."""
        admin = Practitioner(id="adm-002", name="Admin", role=Role.ADMIN)
        original_status = valid_record.status
        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            service.record(valid_record, admin)
        assert valid_record.recorded_by is None
        assert valid_record.recorded_at is None
        assert valid_record.status == original_status


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Negative path – missing mandatory elements
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestMissingMandatoryElements:

    def test_missing_patient_raises_validation_error(
        self, service, valid_record, clinician
    ):
        valid_record.patient = None
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="patient"):
            service.record(valid_record, clinician)

    def test_missing_vaccine_raises_validation_error(
        self, service, valid_record, clinician
    ):
        valid_record.vaccine = None
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="vaccine"):
            service.record(valid_record, clinician)

    def test_missing_occurrence_date_raises_validation_error(
        self, service, valid_record, clinician
    ):
        valid_record.occurrence_date = None
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="occurrence_date"):
            service.record(valid_record, clinician)

    def test_missing_status_raises_validation_error(
        self, service, valid_record, clinician
    ):
        valid_record.status = None
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="status"):
            service.record(valid_record, clinician)

    def test_missing_performer_raises_validation_error(
        self, service, valid_record, clinician
    ):
        valid_record.performer = None
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="performer"):
            service.record(valid_record, clinician)

    def test_all_mandatory_fields_missing_raises_validation_error(
        self, service, clinician
    ):
        """A completely empty record → ValidationError listing all fields."""
        empty = ImmunizationRecord()
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError) as exc_info:
            service.record(empty, clinician)
        msg = str(exc_info.value)
        for field_name in ("patient", "vaccine", "occurrence_date", "status", "performer"):
            assert field_name in msg, f"Expected '{field_name}' in error message"

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", [
        "patient", "vaccine", "occurrence_date", "status", "performer"
    ])
    def test_each_mandatory_field_individually(
        self, service, valid_record, clinician, missing_field
    ):
        """Parametrised: removing each mandatory field triggers ValidationError."""
        setattr(valid_record, missing_field, None)
        with pytest.raises(ValidationError):
            service.record(valid_record, clinician)

    def test_optional_fields_can_be_absent(self, service, valid_record, clinician):
        """Omitting optional fields must not raise."""
        valid_record.lot_number = None
        valid_record.dose_quantity = None
        valid_record.route = None
        valid_record.site = None
        result = service.record(valid_record, clinician)
        assert result.recorded_by == clinician


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Negative path – conflicting state transitions
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestConflictingStateTransitions:

    def test_completed_to_completed_raises_conflict(
        self, service, valid_record, clinician
    ):
        """Re-completing an already COMPLETED immunization is not allowed."""
        valid_record.status = ImmunizationStatus.COMPLETED
        with pytest.raises(ConflictError):
            service.update_status(
                valid_record, ImmunizationStatus.COMPLETED, clinician
            )

    def test_entered_in_error_to_completed_raises_conflict(
        self, service, valid_record, clinician
    ):
        """ENTERED_IN_ERROR is a terminal state; no forward transition allowed."""
        valid_record.status = ImmunizationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR
        with pytest.raises(ConflictError):
            service.update_status(
                valid_record, ImmunizationStatus.COMPLETED, clinician
            )

    def test_entered_in_error_to_not_done_raises_conflict(
        self, service, valid_record, clinician
    ):
        """Cannot revert a retracted immunization."""
        valid_record.status = ImmunizationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR
        with pytest.raises(ConflictError):
            service.update_status(
                valid_record, ImmunizationStatus.NOT_DONE, clinician
            )

    def test_entered_in_error_to_entered_in_error_raises_conflict(
        self, service, valid_record, clinician
    ):
        """Self-transition on terminal state must fail."""
        valid_record.status = ImmunizationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR
        with pytest.raises(ConflictError):
            service.update_status(
                valid_record, ImmunizationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR, clinician
            )

    def test_completed_to_entered_in_error_is_allowed(
        self, service, valid_record, clinician
    ):
        """Valid transition: COMPLETED → ENTERED_IN_ERROR (retraction)."""
        valid_record.status = ImmunizationStatus.COMPLETED
        result = service.update_status(
            valid_record, ImmunizationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR, clinician
        )
        assert result.status == ImmunizationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR

    def test_not_done_to_completed_is_allowed(
        self, service, valid_record, clinician
    ):
        """Valid transition: NOT_DONE → COMPLETED (late recording)."""
        valid_record.status = ImmunizationStatus.NOT_DONE
        result = service.update_status(
            valid_record, ImmunizationStatus.COMPLETED, clinician
        )
        assert result.status == ImmunizationStatus.COMPLETED

    def test_not_done_to_entered_in_error_is_allowed(
        self, service, valid_record, clinician
    ):
        """Valid transition: NOT_DONE → ENTERED_IN_ERROR."""
        valid_record.status = ImmunizationStatus.NOT_DONE
        result = service.update_status(
            valid_record, ImmunizationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR, clinician
        )
        assert result.status == ImmunizationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR

    def test_conflict_error_preserves_original_status(
        self, service, valid_record, clinician
    ):
        """On ConflictError the record status must remain unchanged."""
        valid_record.status = ImmunizationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR
        with pytest.raises(ConflictError):
            service.update_status(
                valid_record, ImmunizationStatus.COMPLETED, clinician
            )
        assert valid_record.status == ImmunizationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR

    def test_non_clinician_cannot_change_status(
        self, service, valid_record
    ):
        """Permission gate is enforced on status updates too."""
        valid_record.status = ImmunizationStatus.COMPLETED
        read_only = Practitioner(id="ro-002", name="Observer", role=Role.READ_ONLY)
        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            service.update_status(
                valid_record, ImmunizationStatus.ENTERED_IN_ERROR, read_only
            )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Edge / boundary tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestEdgeCases:

    def test_midnight_boundary_today_is_accepted(self, service, valid_record, clinician):
        """
        Simulate a record whose occurrence_date equals today at midnight;
        must be accepted (date comparison is date-only, not datetime).
        """
        valid_record.occurrence_date = date.today()
        result = service.record(valid_record, clinician)
        assert result.occurrence_date == date.today()

    def test_clock_mock_still_rejects_future(
        self, service, valid_record, clinician
    ):
        """
        Even if system clock is mocked to a past date, a date beyond that
        mocked 'today' must be rejected.
        """
        mocked_today = date(2023, 1, 1)
        valid_record.occurrence_date = date(2023, 1, 2)   # one day ahead of mock

        with patch("builtins.open"):   # unrelated mock to confirm we patch correctly
            with patch.object(
                type(service),
                "_validate",
                wraps=lambda self, r: (
                    (_ for _ in ()).throw(ValidationError("future"))
                    if r.occurrence_date > mocked_today
                    else None
                ),
            ):
                with pytest.raises(ValidationError, match="future"):
                    service.record(valid_record, clinician)

    def test_immunization_for_newborn(
        self, service, valid_record, clinician, patient
    ):
        """Date of birth as administered date (newborn vaccination) is accepted."""
        valid_record.occurrence_date = date.today()   # born today, vaccinated today
        result = service.record(valid_record, clinician)
        assert result.occurrence_date == date.today()

    def test_duplicate_record_call_stamps_new_recorded_at(
        self, service, valid_record, clinician
    ):
        """
        Calling record() twice on the same object updates recorded_at
        (service doesn't cache stale timestamps).
        """
        first = service.record(valid_record, clinician)
        first_ts = first.recorded_at

        # Reset for second call simulation
        valid_record.recorded_at = None
        valid_record.recorded_by = None

        second = service.record(valid_record, clinician)
        # Both timestamps are valid UTC datetimes
        assert second.recorded_at is not None
        assert second.recorded_at.tzinfo is not None
```

---

## Design Decisions

| Concern | Decision |
|---|---|
| **Test isolation** | Each test owns its own fixture snapshot; `valid_record` is a per-test copy via `@pytest.fixture()` (function scope). |
| **Parametrisation** | Date ranges and missing mandatory fields use `@pytest.mark.parametrize` to avoid copy-paste and improve coverage breadth. |
| **Permission-first ordering** | One test verifies the permission check fires *before* validation, so an attacker cannot probe field validity by trying invalid payloads. |
| **State immutability on failure** | Dedicated assertions confirm that failed operations leave the record in its original state (no partial writes). |
| **Terminal state** | `ENTERED_IN_ERROR` is modelled as a terminal state with no valid outgoing transitions, matching FHIR semantics. |
| **Clock isolation** | The boundary test patches the internal date comparison to show the pattern for CI environments with frozen clocks. |

```python
"""
Pytest tests for Immunization recording requirement:
"A clinician shall record an Immunization with administered date not in the future."

Covers:
- Happy path: valid immunization with past/today dates
- Negative paths: future date, permission denial, missing mandatory elements,
  conflicting state transitions
"""

import pytest
from datetime import date, datetime, timedelta, timezone
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from typing import Optional
from enum import Enum


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain model stubs (replace with real imports in your codebase)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class ImmunizationStatus(str, Enum):
    COMPLETED = "completed"
    ENTERED_IN_ERROR = "entered-in-error"
    NOT_DONE = "not-done"


class Role(str, Enum):
    CLINICIAN = "clinician"
    PATIENT = "patient"
    ADMIN = "admin"
    READ_ONLY = "read_only"


class PermissionError(Exception):
    """Raised when a user lacks the required privilege."""


class ValidationError(Exception):
    """Raised when a resource fails domain validation."""


class ConflictError(Exception):
    """Raised on illegal state transitions."""


@dataclass
class Vaccine:
    code: str           # e.g. CVX code
    display: str


@dataclass
class Patient:
    id: str
    name: str


@dataclass
class Practitioner:
    id: str
    name: str
    role: Role


@dataclass
class ImmunizationRecord:
    patient: Optional[Patient] = None
    vaccine: Optional[Vaccine] = None
    occurrence_date: Optional[date] = None          # administered date
    status: Optional[ImmunizationStatus] = None
    performer: Optional[Practitioner] = None
    lot_number: Optional[str] = None
    dose_quantity: Optional[float] = None           # mL
    route: Optional[str] = None                     # e.g. "IM"
    site: Optional[str] = None                      # e.g. "left deltoid"
    recorded_by: Optional[Practitioner] = None
    recorded_at: Optional[datetime] = None


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Service stub (replace with real service / repository layer)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class ImmunizationService:
    """
    Thin facade; real implementation would persist to FHIR / DB.
    Raises domain exceptions on invalid input.
    """

    REQUIRED_FIELDS = ("patient", "vaccine", "occurrence_date", "status", "performer")

    def record(
        self,
        record: ImmunizationRecord,
        requesting_user: Practitioner,
    ) -> ImmunizationRecord:
        self._check_permission(requesting_user)
        self._validate(record)
        record.recorded_by = requesting_user
        record.recorded_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        return record

    def update_status(
        self,
        existing: ImmunizationRecord,
        new_status: ImmunizationStatus,
        requesting_user: Practitioner,
    ) -> ImmunizationRecord:
        self._check_permission(requesting_user)
        self._check_status_transition(existing.status, new_status)
        existing.status = new_status
        return existing

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # private helpers
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------

    def _check_permission(self, user: Practitioner) -> None:
        if user.role not in (Role.CLINICIAN,):
            raise PermissionError(
                f"User '{user.name}' with role '{user.role}' is not authorised "
                "to record immunizations."
            )

    def _validate(self, record: ImmunizationRecord) -> None:
        # 1. mandatory fields
        missing = [f for f in self.REQUIRED_FIELDS if getattr(record, f) is None]
        if missing:
            raise ValidationError(f"Missing mandatory field(s): {', '.join(missing)}")
